title: OT measure-coupling gap vs conventional gap-function model — CV-1..CV-7 accuracy benchmark
status: CONVERGED (round 3: no CV improved; improvement frontier 7 -> 1 -> 0)

cv1.name: Hertz half-width a_relerr (small load, robust delta-window)

rounds.label: convergence test + Euler A100; math agent corrected a wrong round-1 claim (geometric tangent is path-only, not an accuracy bias)
CV-1 floor test -> CONVERGED
rounds.cv1_mesh_convergence: robust a_err vs n_fine: 120->1.90%, 240->0.50%, 360->0.40%, 480->0.43% (plateau ~0.4-0.5%); Euler --fine-mesh -> 6.90% (degrades). Discrete-edge floor: infinite Hertz edge-pressure slope rounded over 1 element.
rounds.note: No CV improved in round 3 -> stop criterion met.

stop_reason: Converged at round 3: no CV improved (frontier 7->1->0). CV-1 plateau proven by mesh convergence + Euler fine-mesh degradation; the other 6 at evidenced floors since rounds 1-2. Math verification clean (one wrong doc claim self-corrected).

two_body_bvp_extension.label: CV-8 (T2) deformable-deformable OT Hertz + CV-9a (T4) N-body array via the closest-point OT transition map pi_B o phi_A
two_body_bvp_extension.status: VERIFIED (post-fix; orchestrator independently re-ran every gate)
two_body_bvp_extension.regime: contact between two DEFORMABLE bodies (the rigid-master CV-1..7 suite never reaches this; this is the regime OT is built for)
two_body_bvp_extension.unlock: solvers/contact/measure_coupling/two_body.py::assemble_two_body_contact assembles the full 4-block mortar tangent [[K_ss,K_sm],[K_ms,K_mm]] with K_ms=K_sm^T (symmetric SPSD)
two_body_bvp_extension.ot_map_finding: GLOBAL ARCLENGTH MonotoneCoupling1D (Brenier map for two MATED rough profiles) SMEARS a partial/non-conforming contact across the whole interface; the CLOSEST-POINT projection pi_B o phi_A is the correct OT map for a localized convex indenter (convex-cost OT degenerates to g=|p-c|-R, per cv1_ot_gap.py:15-20). Driver hardcodes closest_point for seat/slide/convergence; arclength only for full-contact patch test.
prior_bugs_fixed: inverted Hertz geometry (cv8 block_mesh sign made indenter convex the wrong way)
use of global arclength MonotoneCoupling1D for a partial contact (smears -> mis-maps central nodes to indenter edge)

cv8_T2.p0_note: drops then plateaus ~5.8% (a real floor, not a lucky point)

cv9a_T4.force_balance_note: genuine per-pair |f|~14.4 cancellation, not trivial zero
cv9a_T4.per_component_anisotropy_ring_mesh: 11.59% (genuine artifact, honestly labeled mesh-symmetry; MEAN gate passes under both)

two_body_bvp_extension.honest_residuals: CV-8 ~3-6% in a/p0 is discrete contact-edge noise (infinite Hertz edge-pressure slope rounded over one element; p0 plateaus ~5.8%); both single-realization, plane-strain CST (small-strain Tri2D), frictionless, rigid outer walls (T4); dropped dn/dchi geometric term is the standard small-rotation approximation (recovered across Newton iters).
